objective: Plan the restart, provider removal, update, rollback, restore, cleanup, archive, uninstall, retirement, evidence-bundle, and first-operator proof.
falsifiable_claim: The selected lifecycle reconstructs and retires without hidden authority or lost obligations, and the offline bundle keeps integrity, valid-as-of, and currentness distinct.
exit_criteria: Restart, provider removal, signed update, rollback, restore, cleanup, archive, uninstall, and retire paths pass.
Offline evidence and first-operator metrics pass while integrity, valid-as-of, and currentness remain distinct.
Retained evidence log contains exactly one literal M9_LIFECYCLE_EVIDENCE_OPERATOR_PROOF_EXIT=0; process exit status alone is insufficient.
adversarial_or_fault_proof: Inject corrupted update, rollback, interrupted restore, missing cleanup, provider loss, stale checkpoint, archive replay, uninstall residue, retirement bypass, and operator-study exclusion.
remaining_nonclaims: No production SLA beyond actually closed gates.
Lifecycle success cannot promote authority, currentness, product readiness, or stage status by prose.
source_provenance: Plan-level proposal derived from docs/architecture/_meta/work-items/m0-m14-plan-gap-audit.md at origin/master 61eba1802992c01efa7d3188184ff315ad9d2ba0; status transaction 2026-07-22.
